epics.next_action: Legacy epic — shipped pre-spec-template convention. See spec.md stub + stories.json.
Closed reopen-remediation in f53eacf (AC-039 fix + AC-047 spec amendment). AC-045 scenario 5 / B3 retry-attempts flake tracked as separate finding.
Architectural refactor — run /base:arch-debate first; do not /base:feature directly until the receive-engine boundary is debated.

findings.text: Six of seven React contexts (ChatStore, NostrIdentity, Profile, PollStore, Backup, Marmot) ship substantial state logic with no unit-level guard; only LanguageContext is exercised indirectly via i18n tests.

deferred.detail: epic-sized: 7 contexts/3375 LOC (Marmot 1689), needs multi-story test epic not 1 fix

findings.text: Seven of nine custom hooks (useDirectReactions, useMoodTheme, useOnlineStatus, useSelectedTopics, useStudyTimer, useThemeStyles, useTopicProgress) have no unit test; only useDecryptedImage and useImageSend are covered.

deferred.detail: 3 named hooks deleted (learning platform); 4 remaining are React-lifecycle, need DOM test infra (testing-library/happy-dom, deliberately absent) or refactor

findings.text: base:e2e-testing skill runs in a forked context but returns only 'Skill execution completed' to the parent — no pass/fail counts, no failure messages. Caller has to re-run the runner directly to learn whether tests passed.

DM e2e specs (dm-giftwrap-bell, dm-historical-recovery, dm-third-party-inbound) publish gift-wraps / kind-4 events directly to strfry via raw WebSocket from the test process. Violates the publish-via-app guideline (see CLAUDE.md "E2E tests"): tests pass even when the app's NDK/publish path is broken. Rewrite to publish through a second browser.newContext() that boots the peer and calls the app's publish helper. Open question for dm-third-party-inbound: the app likely has no kind-4 NIP-04 publish path; either confirm an existing UI/bridge route, or document this spec as the narrow exception with a fixture-loader pattern rather than inline WebSocket.

The __nostlingPublishDm test bridge lives in unreadStore.ts but has nothing to do with unread counts (module cohesion smell, flagged in DM walled-garden Opus review). Relocating it out of scope for this epic (risk of regressions). Revisit in a dedicated cleanup pass.

ContactChat uses setInterval with 50ms polling for up to 5s to wait for MarmotContext readiness before applying the walled-garden gate on historical events. A cleaner approach would be a whenReady(): Promise<void> method on MarmotContext that resolves when groups are hydrated. Deferred from DM walled-garden epic per reviewer recommendation (out of scope).
Extract DM profileMap builder; tidy feedback-mode prop coordination in ContactChat
findings.motivation: The feedback channel epic surfaced a pre-existing duplication and some prop-coordination spread. The two-entry DM profileMap (self + one peer) is built near-identically in app/pages/feedback.tsx and app/pages/contacts.tsx ContactDetailView, with the 'own user' entry byte-for-byte identical. ContactChat's feedback-mode behaviour is split across two files (feedback.tsx passes source + composerPlaceholder; ContactChat derives image/reaction gating from source), so the coordinated props can silently drift. None of this is a bug today, but the duplication predates the epic and the feedback page added a third near-copy of the profileMap, so the cost will keep growing as more DM surfaces appear.
findings.behavior: app/pages/feedback.tsx (profileMap ~lines 43-56) and app/pages/contacts.tsx ContactDetailView (profileMap ~lines 220-233) independently construct {[selfHex]:{...}, [peerHex]:{...}} MemberProfile maps; the self entry (nickname: ownProfile.nickname || truncateNpub(pubkeyToNpub(self)), avatar: ownProfile.avatar, updatedAt: new Date().toISOString()) is duplicated verbatim. Separately, ContactChat repeats the 'source === "feedback"' check three times (send markers, onReact gating, allowImageAttachments).
findings.approach: 1) Extract buildDmProfileMap({ selfHex, selfProfile, peerHex, peerNickname, peerAvatar, peerUpdatedAt? }) into a shared module (e.g. app/src/lib/contacts.ts or a profile helper) and call it from both feedback.tsx and contacts.tsx ContactDetailView — this is the high-value, low-risk extraction touching a pre-existing duplication. 2) Optionally hoist 'const isFeedback = source === "feedback"' in ContactChat for readability — but KEEP the three send-path gates (markers, onReact, allowImageAttachments + the paste/drop guards) as separate explicit checks: they guard independent send paths and their separateness is what makes each path auditable (do NOT merge them). 3) Do NOT introduce a <FeedbackChat> wrapper yet — wait for a second feedback-like surface; one call site does not justify the indirection.
findings.success_signal: feedback.tsx and contacts.tsx ContactDetailView both call one buildDmProfileMap helper; no duplicated self-profile literal; the three feedback send-path gates remain separate and explicit.

archive.text: onMembersChanged fires only when currentMembers.length differs from stored.memberPubkeys.length; a future ghost-cleanup pass that removes a ghost but adds a real member in the same commit would leave the departed member visible in the UI even after AC-MEMBERS-1 passes.

Profile-request memo.attempts stays at 0 under Playwright fake-clock cycling — AC-045 scenario 5 deferred (test.fixme). Real cause undiagnosed; candidates: fake-clock + setInterval interaction, recordEmitted Date.now() racing real-time write, or requestedOnEntryForRef cycling under cross-navigation re-mount. Boundary contract already vitest-covered.
archive.reason: Retry-cap behavior (1h/7d/UNANSWERED_MAX_ATTEMPTS=3) is deterministically covered by profileRequestStorage.integration.test.ts (8 tests). The e2e scenario-5 variant is redundant and blocked by an undiagnosed Playwright page.clock+timer interaction (two prior diagnoses already proved wrong); chasing it adds no behavioral coverage. Leave test.fixme in place.

archive.text: groups+dm e2e suite scale-bleed: 177-test single-run cumulative had a 1-test failure at #130 (groups-profile-request.spec.ts:149 scenario 1 'aged-history backfill AC-045'), but the same scenario passes in 2.6s when run in isolation. After ~10min the dev server's HMR cache, strfry's tmpfs event log, and per-context IDB state accumulate enough drift to make subscription-driven assertions miss their deadline. Generalises the scenario-5 finding (profile-request-memo-attempts-stays-at) — same root pattern, different AC-045 scenario surfacing it.
archive.reason: Did not reproduce: the named test (groups-profile-request scenario 1, aged-history AC-045) passed in 9.3s at position #168 of a clean 216-test/26-min full groups run this session (0 failures). Generalises the already-rejected scenario-5 finding; the AC-045 behaviors are deterministically unit-covered (profileRequestStorage.integration.test.ts). Intermittent e2e-infra drift, not a product bug; test-isolation hardening is uncertain-payoff for a non-reproducing flake.

archive.text: ContactChat.tsx uses the same fetch-then-live-sub anti-pattern fixed in welcomeSubscription.ts: Phase-1 runs three parallel fetchEventsWithTimeout calls for DM history then Phase-2 opens three live ndk.subscribe calls, leaving a gap where events are silently lost.
archive.reason: The welcomeSubscription since-anchor fix does NOT transfer: ContactChat's primary DM path is kind-1059 gift-wraps whose outer created_at is randomized/backdated up to 2 days (NIP-59 privacy), so a since:fetchStartedAt filter on the gift-wrap live sub would DROP incoming DMs — a regression. since is only safe on the legacy kind-4 subs (real timestamps), whose inbound-only gap is negligible. Existing per-id dedup (ingestEvent/knownMessageIds + shouldIngestRumor) already absorbs the small overlap. Closing the gap properly would need a relay-replay mechanism that doesn't key on the spoofed gift-wrap timestamp — out of scope for a smell-driven refactor.
